expectations.text: Response designs exactly 3 test cases, each with a distinct concrete capability (not variations of the same thing)
expectations.evidence: Response produces exactly 3 cases: 'basic-fetch-implementation' (URL endpoint construction), 'function-signature-and-return-shape' (TypeScript return type and function usage), 'integration-and-unit-handling' (celsius constraint and conversion awareness). Three distinct capabilities, not variations.

expectations.text: Response writes exactly 4 expectations per test case
expectations.evidence: Eval 1: 4 expectations numbered 1-4. Eval 2: 4 expectations numbered 1-4. Eval 3: 4 expectations numbered 1-4. All labeled 'Evidence-Anchored'.

expectations.text: At least one expectation per test case anchors to a specific identifier from the skill: fetchWeather, temperature_2m, wind_speed_10m, or the exact return shape { temperature, windSpeed, unit: 'celsius' }
expectations.evidence: Eval 1 expectation 2: 'current=temperature_2m,wind_speed_10m'. Eval 1 expectation 4: 'fetchWeather function'. Eval 2 expectation 3: 'unit: 'celsius''. Eval 3 expectation 1: 'unit: 'celsius''. Eval 3 expectation 4: '{ temperature: number, windSpeed: number, unit: 'celsius' }'. All three cases anchor to skill-specific identifiers.

expectations.text: Response explains the evidence-anchoring requirement: that without-skill should fail most expectations, and that expectations must reference skill-specific content not derivable from general LLM knowledge
expectations.evidence: Opening paragraph states: 'exactly 4 evidence-anchored expectations designed to discriminate between general LLM knowledge and skill-specific implementation.' Each expectation is labeled 'Evidence-Anchored:'. The discrimination principle is explicitly stated in the framing.
